original: He shall be like a tree planted by the rivers of water.
english: A rooted life bears fruit in season and does not wither.
spanish: Una vida enraizada da fruto en su tiempo y no se marchita.
recommendedEditionSource: The Book of Psalms, KJV witness — Psalm 1:3
notes: Mode A extraction from uploaded KJV Psalms witness, page 11. Short working rendering; KJV public-domain text, edition design copyrighted.

original: I laid me down and slept; I awaked; for the LORD sustained me.
english: Sleep becomes trust when waking is received, not seized.
spanish: Dormir se vuelve confianza cuando el despertar se recibe, no se conquista.
recommendedEditionSource: The Book of Psalms, KJV witness — Psalm 3:5
notes: Mode A extraction from uploaded KJV Psalms witness, page 12.

original: Commune with your own heart upon your bed, and be still.
english: The night gives the heart back to itself; be still enough to hear it.
spanish: La noche devuelve el corazón a sí mismo; aquieta lo suficiente para oírlo.
recommendedEditionSource: The Book of Psalms, KJV witness — Psalm 4:4
notes: Mode A extraction from uploaded KJV Psalms witness, page 13.

original: What is man, that thou art mindful of him?
english: Under moon and stars, the human question becomes small enough to become real.
spanish: Bajo luna y estrellas, la pregunta humana se vuelve pequeña hasta hacerse real.

original: If the foundations be destroyed, what can the righteous do?
english: When the ground fails, action must discover a deeper ground.
spanish: Cuando falla el suelo, la acción debe descubrir un suelo más profundo.

original: The words of the LORD are pure words: as silver tried in a furnace of earth.

original: How long wilt thou forget me, O LORD? for ever?

original: He that walketh uprightly, and worketh righteousness, and speaketh the truth in his heart.

notes: Mode A extraction from uploaded KJV Psalms witness, page 22.

original: Thou wilt shew me the path of life.

original: Keep me as the apple of the eye, hide me under the shadow of thy wings.

original: The LORD is my rock, and my fortress, and my deliverer.

original: The heavens declare the glory of God; and the firmament sheweth his handiwork.

original: Let the words of my mouth, and the meditation of my heart, be acceptable in thy sight.

original: Some trust in chariots, and some in horses: but we will remember the name of the LORD our God.

original: My God, my God, why hast thou forsaken me?

original: I am poured out like water, and all my bones are out of joint.

original: The LORD is my shepherd; I shall not want.

original: Yea, though I walk through the valley of the shadow of death, I will fear no evil.

original: The LORD is my light and my salvation; whom shall I fear?
